{"content":[{"text":"Title: Programmatic Tool Calling / Code Mode\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/develop/clients/client-best-practices#programmatic-tool-calling-/-code-mode\nPage: docs/develop/clients/client-best-practices\nContent: Programmatic Tool Calling / Code Mode\nWith direct tool calling, every tool invocation is a round trip: the model generates a tool call, the client executes it, and the full result flows back into the model's context. When a task requires chaining multiple tools (read a document, transform it, write it somewhere else), each intermediate result passes through the model, consuming tokens and adding latency even when it has nothing to do with them. Programmatic tool calling (sometimes called \"code mode\") provides a way for clients to compose tool calls effectively. Instead of calling tools directly, the model writes code that calls tools. The code executes in a sandboxed environment, and only the final result returns to the model. Programmatic tool calling is powerful and allows for more efficient use of MCP tools and resources, but requires\nclients to implement a sandbox environment.","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Motivation\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/seps/1686-tasks#motivation\nPage: seps/1686-tasks\nContent: Motivation\nThe current MCP specification supports tool calls that execute a request and eventually receive a response, and tool calls can be passed a progress token to integrate with MCP’s progress-tracking functionality, enabling host applications to receive status updates for a tool call via notifications. However, there is no way for a client to explicitly request the status of a tool call, resulting in states where it is possible for a tool call to have been dropped on the server, and it is unknown if a response or a notification may ever arrive. Similarly, there is no way for a client to explicitly retrieve the result of a tool call after it has completed — if the result was dropped, clients must call the tool again, which is undesirable for tools expected to take minutes or more. This is particularly relevant for MCP servers abstracting existing workflow-based APIs, such as AWS Step Functions, Workflows for Google Cloud, or APIs representing CI/CD pipelines, among other applications. Today, it is possible for individual MCP servers to represent tools in a way that enables this, with certain compromises. For example, a server may expose a long_running_tool and wish to support this pattern, splitting it into three separate tools to accommodate this: start_long_running_tool : This would start the work represented by long_running_tool and return a tracking token of some kind, such as a job ID. get_long_running_tool_status(token) : This would accept the tracking token and return the current status of the tool call, informing the caller that the operation is still ongoing. get_long_running_tool_result(token) : This would accept the tracking token and return the result of the tool call, if it is available. Representing a tool in this way seems to solve for the use case, but it introduces a new problem: Tools are generally-expected to be orchestrated by an agent, and agent-driven polling is both unnecessarily expensive and inconsistent — it relies on prompt engineering to steer an agent to poll at all. In the original long_running_tool case, the client had no way of knowing if a response would ever be received, while in the start_long_running_tool case, the application has no way of knowing if the agent will orchestrate tools according to the specific contract of the server. It is also impossible for the host application to take ownership of this orchestration, as this tool-splitting is both conventions-based and may be implemented in different ways across MCP servers — one server may have three tools for one conceptual operation (as in our example), or it may have more, in the case of more complex, multi-step operations. On the other hand, if active task polling is not needed, existing MCP servers can fully-wrap a workflow API in a single tool call that polls for a result, but this introduces an undesirable implementation cost: an MCP server wrapping an existing workflow API is a server that only exists for polling other systems. Affected Customer Use Cases These concerns are backed by real use cases that Amazon has seen both internally and with their external customers (identities redacted where non-public): 1. Healthcare & Life Sciences Data Analysis Challenge: Amazon’s customers in the healthcare and life sciences industry are attempting to use MCP to wrap existing computational tools to analyze molecular properties and predict drug interactions, processing hundreds of thousands of data points per job from chemical libraries through multiple inference models simultaneously. These complex, multi-step workflows require a way to actively check statuses, as they take upwards of several hours, making retries undesirable.\nCurrent Workaround: Not yet determined.\nImpact: Cannot integrate with real-time research workflows, prevents interactive drug discovery platforms, and blocks automated research pipelines. These customers are looking for best practices for workflow-based tool calls and have noted the lack of first-class support in MCP as a concern. If these customers do not have a solution for long-running tool calls, they will likely forego MCP and continue using their existing platforms.\nIdeal: Concurrent and poll-able tool calls as an answer for operations executing in the range of a few minutes, and some form of push notification system to avoid blocking their agents on long analyses on the order of hours. This SEP supports the former use case, and offers a framework that could extend to support the latter. 2. Enterprise Automation Platforms Challenge: Amazon’s large enterprise customers are looking to develop internal MCP platforms to automate SDLC processes across their organizations, extending to sales, customer service, legal, HR, and cross-divisional teams. They have noted they have long-running agent and agent-tool interactions, supporting complex business process automation.\nCurrent Workaround: Not yet determined. Considering an application-level system outside of MCP backed by webhooks.\nImpact: Limitations related to the host application being unaware of tool execution state prevent complex business process automation and limit sophisticated multi-step operations. These customers want to dispatch processes concurrently and collect their results later, and are noting the lack of explicit late-retrieval as a concern — and are considering involved application-level notification systems as a possible workaround.\nIdeal: Built-in mechanisms for actively checking the status of ongoing work to avoid needing to implement notification systems specific to their own tool conventions themselves. 3. Code Migration Workflows Challenge: Amazon has automated code migration and transformation tools to perform upgrades across its own codebases and those of external customers, and is attempting to wrap those tools in MCP servers. These migrations analyze dependencies, transform code to avoid deprecated runtime features, and validate changes across multiple repositories. These migrations range from minutes to hours depending on migration scope, complexity, and validation requirements.\nCurrent Workaround: Developers implement manual tracking by splitting a job into create and get tools, forcing models to manage state and repeatedly poll for completion.\nImpact: Poor developer experience due to needing to replicate this hand-rolled polling mechanism across many tools. One team had to debug an issue where the model would hallucinate job names if it hadn’t listed them first. Validating that this does not happen across many tools in a large toolset is time-consuming and error-prone.\nIdeal: Support natively polling tool state at the data layer to support pushing a tool to the background and avoiding blocking other tasks in the chat session, while still supporting deterministic polling and result retrieval. The team needs the same pattern across many tools in their MCP servers, and wants a common solution across them, which this SEP directly supports. 4. Test Execution Platforms Challenge: Amazon’s internal test infrastructure executes comprehensive test suites including thousands of cases, integration tests across services, and performance benchmarks. They have built an MCP server wrapping this existing infrastructure.\nCurrent Workaround: For streaming test logs, the MCP server exposes a tool that can read a range of log lines, as it cannot effectively notify the client when the execution is complete. There is not yet any workaround for executing test runs.\nImpact: Cannot run a test suite and stream its logs simultaneously without a single hours-long tool call, which would time out on either the client or the server. This prevents agents from looking into test failures in an incomplete test run until the entire test suite has completed, potentially hours later.\nIdeal: Support host application-driven tool polling for intermediate results, so a client can be notified when a long-running tool is complete. This SEP does not fully-support this use case (it does enable polling), but the Task execution model can be extended to do so, as discussed in the “Future Work” section. 5. Deep Research Challenge: Deep research tools spawn multiple research agents to gather and summarize information about topics, going through several rounds of search and conversation turns internally to produce a final result for the caller application. The tool takes an extended amount of time to execute, and it is not always clear if the tool is still executing.\nCurrent Workaround: The research tool is split into a separate create tool to create a report job and a get tool to get the status/result of that job later.\nImpact: When using this with host applications, the agent sometimes runs into issues calling the get tool repeatedly — in particular, it calls the tool once before ending its conversation turn, claiming to be \"waiting\" before calling the tool again. It cannot resume until receiving a new user message. This also complicates expiration times, as it is not possible to predict when the client will retrieve the result when this occurs. It is possible to work around this by adding a wait tool for the model, but this prevents the model from doing anything else concurrently.\nIdeal: Support polling a tool call’s state in a deterministic way and notify the model when a result is ready, so the tool result can be immediately retrieved and deleted from the server. Other than notifying the model (a host application concern), this SEP fully supports this use case. 6. Agent-to-Agent Communication (Multi-Agent Systems) Challenge: One of Amazon’s internal multi-agent systems for customer question answering faces scenarios where agents require significant processing time for complex reasoning, research, or analysis. When agents communicate through MCP, slow agents cause cascading delays throughout this system, as agents are forced to wait on their peers to complete their work.\nCurrent Workaround: Not yet determined.\nImpact: Communication pattern creates cascading delays, prevents parallel agent processing, and degrades system responsiveness for other time-sensitive interactions.\nIdeal: Some method to allow agents to perform other work concurrently and get notified once long-running tasks complete. This SEP supports this use case by enabling host applications to implement background polling for select tool calls without blocking agents. These use cases demonstrate that a mechanism to actively track tool calls and defer results is a real requirement for these types of MCP deployments in production environments. Integration with Existing Architectures Many workflow-driven systems already provide active execution-tracking capabilities with built-in status metadata, monitoring, and data retention policies. This proposal enables MCP servers to expose these existing APIs with thin MCP wrappers while maintaining their existing reliability. Benefits for Existing Architectures: Leverage Existing State Management: Systems like AWS Step Functions, Workflows for Google Cloud, and CI/CD platforms already maintain execution state, logs, and results. MCP servers can expose these systems' existing APIs without pushing the responsibility of polling to a fallible agent. Preserve Native Monitoring: Existing monitoring, alerting, and observability tools continue to work unchanged. The execution happens almost entirely within the existing workflow-management system. Reduce Implementation Overhead: Server implementers don't need to build new state management, persistence, or monitoring infrastructure. They can focus on the MCP protocol mapping of their existing APIs to tasks. This SEP simplifies integration with existing workflows and allows workflow services to continue to manage their own state while delivering a quality customer experience, rather than offloading to agent-polling or building MCP servers that do nothing but poll other services.","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Allow client to call the server’s tools by itself in an agentic loop\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/seps/1577--sampling-with-tools#allow-client-to-call-the-server’s-tools-by-itself-in-an-agentic-loop\nPage: seps/1577--sampling-with-tools\nContent: Allow client to call the server’s tools by itself in an agentic loop\nFrom the server’s perspective, that would remove the need to call tools by itself / inject tool results in follow up sampling calls. The MCP server would just allowlist its own tools in the sampling request, w/t a dedicated tool definition such as: Pros: Safe, limited to that server’s tools. If we propagate the mcp-session-id, can leverage keep any server-side session context / caching","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Calling Tools\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/draft/server/tools#calling-tools\nPage: specification/draft/server/tools\nContent: Calling Tools\nTo invoke a tool, clients send a tools/call request: Request: Response:","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Calling Tools\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/server/tools#calling-tools\nPage: specification/2025-03-26/server/tools\nContent: Calling Tools\nTo invoke a tool, clients send a tools/call request: Request: Response:","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Calling Tools\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/server/tools#calling-tools\nPage: specification/2024-11-05/server/tools\nContent: Calling Tools\nTo invoke a tool, clients send a tools/call request: Request: Response:","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Calling Tools\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/server/tools#calling-tools\nPage: specification/2025-06-18/server/tools\nContent: Calling Tools\nTo invoke a tool, clients send a tools/call request: Request: Response:","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Calling Tools\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/server/tools#calling-tools\nPage: specification/2025-11-25/server/tools\nContent: Calling Tools\nTo invoke a tool, clients send a tools/call request: Request: Response:","type":"text"},{"text":"Title: Understanding the Tool Execution Request\nLink: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/learn/architecture#understanding-the-tool-execution-request\nPage: docs/learn/architecture\nContent: Understanding the Tool Execution Request\nThe tools/call request follows a structured format that ensures type safety and clear communication between client and server. primary agent will not have any tool calls beyond spinning up a subagent!\nfunction M.openai_tools(coordinator_only)\n    -- * inject system message instructions based on available tools\n    local function _openai_tools(coordinator_only)\n        ---@type OpenAITool[]\n        local tools = {}\n        local system_instructs = {}\n\n        if coordinator_only then\n            log:info(\"COORDINATOR_ONLY\")\n            -- * coordinator only gets the \"agents\" tool (for spawning subagents)\n            local delegate_tool = mcp.tools_available[\"delegate\"]\n            if delegate_tool then\n                table.insert(tools, mcp.openai_tool(delegate_tool))\n            end\n            return tools, system_instructs\n        end\n\n        for name, mcp_tool in pairs(mcp.tools_available) do\n            table.insert(tools, mcp.openai_tool(mcp_tool))\n            local tool_instructs = mcp.get_system_message_instructions(name)\n            if tool_instructs then\n                table.insert(system_instructs, tool_instructs)\n            end\n        end\n\n        for _, inprocess_tool in pairs(inprocess.tools_available) do\n            -- PRN push this into inprocess module like mcp/init.lua above?\n\n            if inprocess_tool[\"function\"].name == \"semantic_grep\" then\n                -- somewhat strange but neovim invocations are rooted in the current buffer,\n                -- so I have to use that to dictate some tool availability even though agent‑like\n                -- requests are buffer independent... since my LS client is tied to a buffer in\n                -- neovim, gotta roll with it! ToolCallOnProgress\nfunction M.send_tool_call_router(tool_call, callback, on_progress)\n    local tool_name = tool_call[\"function\"].name\n\n    local function safe_call(fn)\n        -- treat as tool call failure, that way model can choose how to recover... vs just killing your tool runner :)\n        local ok, err = pcall(fn)\n        if not ok then\n            callback(plumbing.create_tool_call_output_for_error_message(err))\n        end\n    end\n\n    local function safe_on_progress(...)\n        -- right now, all mcp tool calls flow through here\n        -- so this feels like a good spot to wrap `on_progress`\n        -- plus I am doing that with `callback` here too\n        --\n        -- also by setting this up here, in MCP land I can require on_progress for all tool calls and this will just silently ignore progress if a tool doesn't want to use it\n        if not on_progress then\n            log:warn(string.format(\"Tool [%s] progress received but there's no on_progress registered: %s\", tool_name, vim.inspect({ ... })))\n            return\n        end\n        local ok, err = pcall(on_progress, ...)\n        if not ok then\n            log:error(string.format(\"Tool [%s] on_progress callback error: %s\", tool_name, err))\n        end\n    end\n\n    if mcp.handles_tool(tool_name) then\n        safe_call(function() mcp.send_tool_call(tool_call, callback, safe_on_progress) end)\n        return\n    end\n\n    if inprocess.handles_tool(tool_name) then\n        safe_call(function() inprocess.send_tool_call(tool_call, callback) end)\n        return\n    end\n\n    callback(plumbing.create_tool_call_output_for_error_message(\"Invalid tool name: \" .. tool_name))\nend\n\nreturn M\n"}]}

 if name then\n                            lines:append_text(name .. \": \" .. text)\n                        else\n                            lines:append_text(text)\n                        end\n Now I have a solid understanding of the architecture. Let me propose a solution and implement it.

## Proposed Design

**Progress display strategy:** Show a "currently running" section under each tool call, with a compact history of recent progress messages. When the tool finishes, that section gets replaced with the tool result.

**Data flow:**
1. MCP server sends `notifications/progress` → dispatched to `progress_callbacks_by_token` → wrapped by `safe_on_progress` → routed to `on_tool_progress` in frontend
2. `on_tool_progress` appends message to `tool_call.progress_messages` → calls `update_chat_viewer_buffer()` → formatter renders progress

**UI presentation:** For each in-progress tool call, show:
- A header: `🔄 Running: tool_name` (yellow highlight)
- Up to 3 most recent progress messages (folded, dimmed)
- Animated spinner that cycles through `⠋ ⠙ ⠹ ⠸ ⠼ ⠴ ⠦ ⠧ ⠇ ⠏`

--- FYI MUST MIRROR OpeanAI format b/c this will get sent back
---   i.e. that means function is a nested object
---@class ToolCall : OpenAIChoiceDeltaToolCall
---
---  my additions:
---@field response_message table|nil -- TODO type this? response message to send call results back to the model that will, also attached to agent_trace (history)
---@field call_output? ToolCallOutput -- tool call outputs (specific to a given tool type, some have standard structure, i.e. MCP)
---@field progress_messages string[] -- progress messages received during tool execution
local ToolCall = {}

---@return ToolCall
function ToolCall:new(o)
    o = o or {}
    o.id = o.id or ""
    o.progress_messages = o.progress_messages or {}
    setmetatable(o, self)
    self.__index = self
    return o
end

---@param message string
function ToolCall:add_progress_message(message)
    table.insert(self.progress_messages, message)
end

---@return boolean
function ToolCall:is_done()
    -- FYI see run_tools_and_send_results_back_to_the_model() for when call_output is set
    return self.call_output ~= nil
end

function ToolCall:is_outstanding()
    return self.response_message == nil
end

-- FYI marker interface for now

return ToolCall
EOF

Now update the generic formatter to show progress messages:

<tool_call>
<function=run_process>
<parameter=command_line>
cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/agents/viewer/formatters/generic.lua << 'HEREDOC'
local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local HLGroups = require("ask-openai.hlgroups")
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

local M = {}

function try_decode_json_string(json_str, message)
    if message:is_still_streaming() then
        return json_str
    end

    local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json(json_str)
    if ok and type(decoded) == "table" then
        return vim.inspect(decoded, { newline = "", indent = "  " })
    end
    return json_str
end

---@param content string
---@param language? string
---@return string
local function code(content, language)
    local lang_prefix = language and #language > 0 and language or ""
    return "```" .. lang_prefix .. "\n" .. content .. "\n```"
end

---@param args string
---@param message RxAccumulatedMessage
---@return string text -- note, not lines
local function handle_apply_patch_args(args, message)
    if message:is_done_streaming() then
        -- FYI this is the only time to log failures, when done streaming:
        local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json(args)
        if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
            return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
        end
        -- * fallback is to show unparsed, final args
        -- also add a warning that will show in the chat window
        return args .. "\n\nFailed to decode as JSON"
    end

    local json_prefix = args:match('^{%s*"patch"%s*:%s*"')
    if not json_prefix then
        return args
    end

    -- * try to complete the JSON string and decode it
    -- assumption is, if final '\"}' is present then it would be done streaming (sans maybe one delta?)
    local try_finish_args = args .. '"}'
    local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json_ignore_failure(try_finish_args)
    if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
        return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
    else
        -- * fallback => try parse as-is
        local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json_ignore_failure(args)
        if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
            return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
        end
        -- * fallback => return unparsed
        return args
    end
end

---@param lines LinesBuilder
---@param tool_call ToolCall
---@param progress_messages string[]
---@param is_done boolean
local function render_progress(lines, tool_call, progress_messages, is_done)
    if is_done then
        return
    end

    -- Show current progress message (most recent) with spinner
    local spinner_chars = { "⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏" }
    local current_time = vim.loop.hrtime()
    local spinner_index = math.floor((current_time / 100000000) % #spinner_chars) + 1
    local spinner_char = spinner_chars[spinner_index]

    -- Show header with tool name and spinner
    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name or "unknown_tool"
    lines:append_line(string.format("%s ⏳ Running: %s", spinner_char, func_name))

    -- Show recent progress messages (up to 3 most recent)
    local num_progress = #progress_messages
    if num_progress > 0 then
        local max_show = math.min(3, num_progress)
        local start_idx = num_progress - max_show + 1

        if max_show < num_progress then
            lines:append_line(string.format("  ... (%d more messages)", num_progress - max_show))
        end

        for i = start_idx, num_progress do
            local msg = progress_messages[i]
            -- Truncate very long messages to avoid overwhelming the view
            if #msg > 200 then
                msg = msg:sub(1, 197) .. "..."
            end
            lines:append_line(string.format("    ↳ %s", msg))
        end
    else
        lines:append_line("    ↳ Waiting for response...")
    end
end

---@type ToolCallFormatter
function M.format(lines, tool_call, message)
    -- if message:is_still_streaming() then
    --     -- TODO?
    --     -- TODO OR message:get_lifecycle_step() == RxAccumulatedMessage.RX_LIFECYCLE.FINISHED
    --     return
    -- end

    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name
    local tool_header = func_name or ""

    local hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_SUCCESS
    if tool_call.call_output then
        if tool_call.call_output.result.isError then
            tool_header = "❌ " .. tool_header
            hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_FAILED
        else
            tool_header = "✅ " .. tool_header
        end
    end
    lines:append_styled_text(tool_header, hl_group)

    -- * tool args
    local args = tool_call["function"].arguments
    if args then
        if func_name == "apply_patch" then
            lines:append_text(handle_apply_patch_args(args, message))
        else
            lines:append_text(try_decode_json_string(args, message))
        end
    end
    -- PRN mark outputs somehow? or just dump them? (I hate to waste space)

    -- * progress messages (shown when tool is still running)
    local is_tool_done = tool_call:is_done()
    render_progress(lines, tool_call, tool_call.progress_messages or {}, is_tool_done)

    if not is_tool_done then
        return
    end

    -- TODO add failure recovery to building messages for logs... do not kill things b/c a formatter failed!
    --  allow agent to continue even if formatters are FUUUUU

    local is_mcp_like_output = tool_call.call_output and tool_call.call_output:is_mcp()
    if is_mcp_like_output then
        ---@type MCP_ContentBlock[]
        local content = tool_call.call_output.result.content

        local multiple_outputs = #content > 1
        for _, output in ipairs(content) do
            local name = output.name
            local text = tostring(output.text or "")
            if not text then
                -- PRN log and/or skip?
                if name then
                    lines:append_text(name)
                else
                    lines:append_text("[ empty text ]")
                end
            elseif name == "STDOUT" then
                if text then
                    lines:append_text_fold_if_long("STDOUT", text)
                end
            else
                -- GENERIC output type
                if (name == nil or name == "") and multiple_outputs then
                    name = "[NO NAME] and multiple outputs" -- heads up for now so I can identify scenarios/tools, can remove this later
                end
                if output.type == "text" then
                    local is_multi_line = text:match("\n")
                    if is_multi_line then
                        if name then
                            lines:append_text(name .. ":")
                        end
                        lines:append_text(text)
                    else
                        -- single line
                        if name then
                            lines:append_text(name .. ": " .. text)
                        else
                            lines:append_text(text)
                        end
                    end
                else
                    lines:append_unexpected_text("  UNEXPECTED type: \n" .. vim.inspect(output))
                end
            end
        end
    else
        -- TODO NON-MCP tool responses
        --  i.e. in-process tools: rag_query, apply_patch
    end
end

return M
HEREDOC
</parameter>
</function>

aren't in repo root and yet the calculation for number of levels deep returned 0???, check logic for levels deep warning\", vim.log.levels.WARN)\n            end\n        end\n        tool_instructs = tool_instructs:gsub(\"INSERT_CWD\", cwd_text)\n        system = system .. \"\\n\\n\" .. tool_instructs\n\n        local tool_provided_instructs\n        -- Pass coordinator flag based on slash command \"coordinator\"\n        local coordinator_flag = context.includes.coordinator or false\n        tool_definitions, tool_provided_instructs = tool_router.openai_tools(coordinator_flag)\n        if tool_provided_instructs then\n            system = system .. \"\\n\\n\" .. table.concat(tool_provided_instructs, \"\\n\")\n        end\n\n        -- If readonly mode, remove editing tools like apply_patch\n        if context.includes.readonly then\n            local filtered = {}\n            for _, tool in ipairs(tool_definitions) do\n                local name = tool[\"function\"] and tool[\"function\"].name or nil\n                if name ~= \"apply_patch\" then\n                    table.insert(filtered, tool)\n                end\n            end\n            tool_definitions = filtered\n        end\n    end\n\n    -- * display system message in chat window\n    if not first_turn_ns_id then\n        first_turn_ns_id = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace(\"ask.marks.chat.window.first.turn\")\n    end\n    local lines = LinesBuilder:new(first_turn_ns_id)\n    local is_new_trace = AgentsFrontend.trace ~= nil\n    if is_new_trace then\n        -- FYI some previous extmarks are \"dropped\", fine by me to \"turn off the colors\"... but, probably want it for all previous chat extmarks\n        lines:append_styled_lines({ \"--- New Trace Started ---\" }, HLGroups.SYSTEM_PROMPT)\n        -- or:   AgentsFrontend.clear_chat_command()\n    end\n    lines:mark_next_line(HLGroups.SYSTEM_PROMPT)\n    lines:append_folded_styled_text(\"system\\n\" .. system, \"\")\n\n\n    -- * display user message in chat window\n    lines:append_role_header(\"user\")\n    lines:append_text(cleaned_prompt)\n\n    local user_message = cleaned_prompt\n    local code_context = nil\n    if selection then\n        local file_name = files.get_file_relative_path(code_bufnr)\n        -- include line range in the filename like foo.py:10-20\n        local start_line = selection:start_line_1indexed()\n        local end_line = selection:end_line_1indexed()\n        local line_info = start_line == end_line and tostring(start_line) or (start_line .. \"-\" .. end_line)\n        local file_display = file_name .. \":\" .. line_info\n        code_context =\n            \"Here is the code I selected:\" .. \"\\n```\" .. file_display .. \"\\n\" .. selection.original_text .. \"\\n```\"\n\n        -- PRN count \\n in selection.original_text and only fold if > 10\n        local fold = false -- = newline_count > 10\n        if fold then\n            lines:append_folded_styled_text(code_context, \"\")\n        else\n            lines:append_styled_text(code_context, \"\")\n        end\n        user_message = user_message .. \"\\n\\n\" .. code_context\n    end\n\n    if context.includes.current_file then\n        local entire_file_message = MessageBuilder:new()\n            :plain_text(\"FYI, here is my current buffer in Neovim. Use this as context for my request:\")\n            :md_current_buffer(code_bufnr)\n            :to_text()\n\n        -- skip code_context if entire file selected (user intent matters, entire file is vague)\n        lines:append_folded_styled_text(entire_file_message, \"\")\n        user_message = user_message .. \"\\n\\n\" .. entire_file_message\n    end\n\n    lines:append_blank_line()\n    AgentsFrontend.chat_window:append_styled_lines(lines)\n\n    ---@type OpenAIChatCompletion_TxChatMessage[]\n    local messages = {\n        TxChatMessage:system(system)\n    }\n\n    -- ? context.includes.open_files\n    if context.includes.yanks and context.yanks then\n        -- PRN anything I want to show about auto context? (not just yanks)\n        table.insert(messages, TxChatMessage:user_context(context.yanks.content))\n    end\n    if context.includes.commits and context.commits then\n        for _, commit in pairs(context.commits) do\n            table.insert(messages, TxChatMessage:user_context(commit.content))\n        end\n    end\n    if context.includes.project and context.project then\n        -- TODO does any of this belong in the system_message?\n        --  ? actually test if repeating some of this here helps\n        --    i.e. my global project instructions include not touching unrelated code too\n        --  perhaps leave the system_message for coding instructions specific to AskAgent...?\n        vim.iter(context.project)\n            :each(function(value)\n                table.insert(messages, TxChatMessage:user_context(value.content))\n            end)\n    end\n\n    local function then_add_seed_user_messages(rag_matches)\n        local rag_message = rag_instructions.semantic_grep_user_message(rag_matches)\n        if rag_message then\n            table.insert(messages, rag_message)\n        end\n\n        -- * user request should be last\n        -- FYI I had this before RAG matches and it was working fine too\n        table.insert(messages, TxChatMessage:user(user_message))\n\n        local body_overrides = model_params.new_gptoss_chat_body_llama_server({\n            -- local body_overrides = model_params.new_qwen3coder_llama_server_chat_body({\n            messages = messages,\n            model = \"\", -- irrelevant for llama-server\n            tools = tool_definitions,\n        }, context)\n\n        local new_trace = AgentTrace:new(body_overrides, AgentsFrontend.base_url)\n        AgentsFrontend.trace = new_trace -- FYI `.trace` is intended for rare circumstances only, i.e. cancel action which has no context to pass a trace\n        -- log:info(\"sending\", vim.inspect(AgentsFrontend.trace))\n        AgentsFrontend.then_get_assistant_response(new_trace)\n    end\n\n    -- log:error(\"context.includes\", vim.inspect(context.includes))\n    if api.is_rag_enabled() and not context.includes.norag and rag_client.is_rag_supported_in_current_file(code_bufnr) then\n        local this_request_ids, cancel -- declare in advance for closure\n\n        ---@param rag_matches LSPRankedMatch[]\n        function on_rag_response(rag_matches)\n            log:info(\"on_rag_response\")\n            -- * make sure prior (canceled) rag request doesn't still respond\n            if AgentsFrontend.rag_request_ids ~= this_request_ids then\n                log:trace(\"possibly stale rag results, skipping: \" .. vim.inspect({\n                    global_rag_request_ids = AgentsFrontend.rag_request_ids,\n                    this_request_ids = this_request_ids,\n                }))\n                return\n            end\n\n            if AgentsFrontend.rag_cancel == nil then\n                log:error(\"rag appears canceled, skipping on_rag_response...\")\n                return\n            end\n\n            then_add_seed_user_messages(rag_matches)\n        end\n\n        this_request_ids, cancel = rag_client.context_query_for_agents(code_bufnr, cleaned_prompt, code_context, context.includes.top_k, on_rag_response)\n        AgentsFrontend.rag_cancel = function()\n            log:warn(\"canceling RAG\")\n            AgentsFrontend.rag_cancel = nil\n            cancel()\n            AgentsFrontend.rag_request_ids = nil\n        end\n        AgentsFrontend.rag_request_ids = this_request_ids\n    else\n        AgentsFrontend.rag_cancel = nil\n        AgentsFrontend.rag_request_ids = nil\n        then_add_seed_user_messages({})\n    end\nend\n\n---@param trace AgentTrace\nfunction AgentsFrontend.then_get_assistant_response(trace)\n    -- * conversation turns (track start line for streaming chunks)\n    AgentsFrontend.this_turn_chat_start_line_base0 = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer:get_line_count()\n    -- log:info(\"M.this_turn_chat_start_line_base0\", M.this_turn_chat_start_line_base0)\n\n    local next_request = CurlRequestForTrace:new({\n        body = trace:next_curl_request_body(),\n        base_url = trace.base_url,\n        endpoint = CompletionsEndpoints.oai_v1_chat_completions,\n        type = \"agents\",\n    })\n    log:luaify_trace(\"body:\", next_request.body)\n    curl.spawn(next_request, AgentsFrontend)\n    trace:set_last_request(next_request)\nend\n\nfunction AgentsFrontend.abort_and_close()\n    AgentsFrontend.abort_request()\n    if AgentsFrontend.chat_window ~= nil then\n        AgentsFrontend.chat_window:close()\n    end\nend\n\n---@type ExplainError\nfunction AgentsFrontend.explain_error(text)\n    vim.schedule(function()\n        -- TEST this with:\n        -- 1. remove --jinja from llama-server service\n        -- 2. restart service\n        -- 3. try using tools\n        -- =>  curl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 500\n        -- 4. add extra log to confirm:\n        -- log:warn(\"MAKE SURE THIS IS FAILURE PATH\")\n        --\n        -- ALSO 503 error for model loading:\n        --   error = { code = 503, message = \"Loading model\", type = \"unavailable_error\" }\n        AgentsFrontend.chat_window:explain_error(text)\n    end)\nend\n\nfunction _G.MyAgentWindowFolding()\n    local line_num_base1 = vim.v.lnum -- confirmed this is base 1 (might get lnum=0 if no lines though)\n    local fold_value = _G.MyAgentWindowFoldingForLine(line_num_base1)\n\n    -- To force re-evaluate folding on all lines:\n    --   `zx` ***\n    --   can also close (F8) the floating chat window and <leader>ao to reopen\n    -- If nvim isn't re-evaluating some lines, then some folds will appear wrong/partial when they are correct\n    --   this can happen if you set fold ranges AFTER adding/modifying relevant lines\n    --   always update the folds first, then the lines\n    --\n    -- BTW this log entry is designed to see WHEN a line's expr() is evaluated!\n    -- log:info(\"  foldexpr() line[\" .. line_num_base1 .. \"] → \" .. fold_value)\n\n    return fold_value\nend\n\nfunction _G.MyAgentWindowFoldingForLine(line_num_base1)\n    -- * HUGE WIN => with expr I can fold one line only! (IIUC manual is minimum 2)\n    --   * so for long reasoning lines (that wrap but don't span multiple new lines) these can still be collapsed!!\n    --\n    -- FYI read docs about return values for expr:\n    --   https://neovim.io/doc/user/fold.html#fold-expr\n    local folds = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer.folds or {}\n    for _, fold in ipairs(folds) do\n        if line_num_base1 >= fold.start_line_base1 and line_num_base1 <= fold.end_line_base1 then\n            return '1' -- inside first level fold\n        end\n    end\n    return '0' -- this line is not in a fold\nend\n\nfunction AgentsFrontend.clear_undos()\n    -- wipe undo history\n    -- i.e. after assistant response - undo fucks up the extmarks, and the assistant response is not gonna be sent back if modified (this is view only) so just default to making that UX a bit more intuitive\n\n    local previous_undo_level = vim.bo.undolevels\n    vim.bo.undolevels = -1\n    vim.cmd(\"normal! a \") -- no-op edit to commit the change\n    vim.cmd(\"undo\") -- clear old tree\n    vim.bo.undolevels = previous_undo_level\nend\n\nfunction AgentsFrontend.ensure_chat_window_is_open()\n    if AgentsFrontend.chat_window == nil then\n        AgentsFrontend.chat_window = AgentWindow:new()\n\n        -- stop generation, if still wanna look at it w/o closing the window\n        vim.keymap.set(\"n\", \"<Esc>\", AgentsFrontend.abort_request, { buffer = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer_number })\n\n        -- I already use this globally to close a window (:q) ... so just add stop to it:\n        vim.keymap.set(\"n\", \"<F8>\", AgentsFrontend.abort_and_close, { buffer = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer_number })\n\n        vim.keymap.set({ \"n\", \"i\" }, \"<C-s>\", AgentsFrontend.follow_up_command, { buffer = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer_number })\n    end\n\n    AgentsFrontend.chat_window:open()\nend\n\n---@param trace AgentTrace\nlocal function update_chat_viewer_buffer(trace)\n    local request = trace.last_request\n\n    local lines = LinesBuilder:new()\n    for _, rx_message in ipairs(request.accumulated_model_response_messages or {}) do\n        -- FYI !! now it is obvious that this is only operating on accumulated message type!\n\n        -- * message contents\n        local content = rx_message.content or \"\"\n        local reasoning_content = rx_message.reasoning_content or \"\"\n\n        if content ~= \"\" or reasoning_content ~= \"\" then\n            -- ONLY add role header IF there is content (or reasoning) to show... otherwise just show tool_call(s)\n            lines:append_role_header(rx_message.role)\n\n            lines:append_folded_styled_text(reasoning_content, HLGroups.CHAT_REASONING)\n\n            lines:append_text(content)\n            lines:append_blank_line_if_last_is_not_blank() -- only if reasoning doesn't have trailing \\n\n        elseif #rx_message.tool_calls == 0 then\n            -- gptoss120b - this works:\n            --   :AskAgent testing a request, I need you to NOT say anything in response, just stop immediatley\n            lines:append_text(\"[unexpected: empty response]\")\n            lines:append_blank_line()\n        end\n\n        for _, tool_call in ipairs(rx_message.tool_calls) do\n            local function_name = tool_call[\"function\"].name or \"\"\n            local formatter = formatters.get_formatter(function_name)\n            local ok, err = pcall(function() formatter(lines, tool_call, rx_message) end)\n            if not ok then\n                lines:append_unexpected_text(\"Formatter error: \" .. tostring(err))\n                lines:append_text(vim.inspect(tool_call))\n            end\n            lines:append_blank_line_if_last_is_not_blank()\n        end\n    end\n\n    vim.schedule(function()\n        lines.marks_ns_id = request.marks_ns_id -- ?? generate namespace here in lines builder? lines:gen_mark_ns()? OR do it on first downstream use?\n        AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer:replace_with_styled_lines_after(AgentsFrontend.this_turn_chat_start_line_base0, lines)\n    end)\nend\n\n--- think of this as denormalizing SSEs => into aggregate RxAccumulatedMessage\n---@param choice OpenAIChoice|nil\n---@param request CurlRequestForTrace\n---@param sse_parsed OnParsedSSE\nfunction AgentsFrontend.on_streaming_delta_update_message_history(choice, request, sse_parsed)\n    -- *** this is a DENORMALIZER (AGGREGATOR) - CQRS style\n    -- rebuilds message as if sent `stream: false`\n    -- for message history / follow up\n\n    if choice == nil or choice.delta == nil then\n        log:trace(\"[WARN] skipping b/c choice/choice.delta is nil: '\" .. vim.inspect(choice) .. \"'\")\n        return\n    end\n\n    -- * lookup or create message\n    -- FYI this is not well vetted for multi message responses, in fact is this using choice.index for message.index?!\n    --   that said, one message per request is it... unless I am doing something funky with the raw prompt to trigger mulitple messages?\n    local index_base1 = choice.index + 1\n\n    local rx_accum_message = request.accumulated_model_response_messages[index_base1]\n    if rx_accum_message == nil then\n        rx_accum_message = RxAccumulatedMessage:new(choice.delta.role, \"\")\n        rx_accum_message.index = choice.index\n        rx_accum_message._verbatim_content = \"\"\n        -- assumes contiguous indexes, s/b almost always 0 index only, 1 too with dual tool call IIRC (gptoss doesn't do dual tool at once)\n        request.accumulated_model_response_messages[index_base1] = rx_accum_message\n    end\n\n    if sse_parsed.timings then\n        rx_accum_message.timings = sse_parsed.timings\n    end\n\n    if choice.delta.content ~= nil and choice.delta.content ~= vim.NIL then\n        -- by tracking _verbatim_content, I can trim the end every single time\n        -- and if it is not a full match it will show back up once it's past the match point\n        rx_accum_message._verbatim_content = (rx_accum_message._verbatim_content or \"\") .. choice.delta.content\n    end\n\n    if choice.delta.reasoning_content ~= nil and choice.delta.reasoning_content ~= vim.NIL then\n        rx_accum_message.reasoning_content =\n            (rx_accum_message.reasoning_content or \"\") .. choice.delta.reasoning_content\n    end\n\n    if choice.finish_reason ~= nil then\n        -- FYI this is vim.NIL on first too\n        rx_accum_message.finish_reason = choice.finish_reason -- on last delta per index/role (aka message)\n    end\n\n    -- * strip leaked tool call tokens (bug in llama.cpp)\n    -- TODO this is an old bug, s/b resolved... is it ok to remove this?\n    rx_accum_message.content = rx_accum_message._verbatim_content:gsub(\"\\n<tool_call>\\n<function=[%w_]+\", \"\")\n    if rx_accum_message.content ~= rx_accum_message._verbatim_content then\n        log:error(\"stripping LEAKED TOOL CALL!\")\n    end\n\n    local calls = choice.delta.tool_calls\n    if not calls then\n        return\n    end\n\n    -- * parse tool calls (streaming)\n    for _, call_delta in ipairs(calls) do\n        -- * lookup or create new parsed_call\n        local parsed_call = rx_accum_message.tool_calls[call_delta.index + 1]\n        if parsed_call == nil then\n            -- create ToolCall to populate across SSEs\n            parsed_call = ToolCall:new {\n                -- assume these fields are always on first SSE for each tool call\n                id    = call_delta.id,\n                index = call_delta.index,\n                type  = call_delta.type,\n            }\n            table.insert(rx_accum_message.tool_calls, parsed_call)\n        end\n\n        local func = call_delta[\"function\"] -- FYI \"function\" is keyword (lua)\n        if func ~= nil then\n            parsed_call[\"function\"] = parsed_call[\"function\"] or {}\n\n            -- * function.name is entirely in first delta (in my testing)\n            if func.name ~= nil then\n                --   => if that changes, add unit tests to verify observed splits\n                parsed_call[\"function\"].name = func.name\n            end\n\n            -- * funtion.arguments is split across deltas\n            if func.arguments ~= nil then\n                -- accumuluate each chunk\n                parsed_call[\"function\"].arguments =\n                    (parsed_call[\"function\"].arguments or \"\")\n                    .. func.arguments\n            end\n        end\n    end\nend\n\n---@type OnParsedSSE\nfunction AgentsFrontend.on_sse_llama_server_timings(sse)\n    -- PRN use this to extract timing like in rewrites\nend\n\n---@type OnParsedSSE\nfunction AgentsFrontend.on_parsed_data_sse(sse_parsed)\n    -- FYI right now this is desingned for /v1/chat/completions only\n    --   I added this guard based on review of on-on_streaming_delta_update_message_history that appears (IIRC) to be using /v1/chat/completions ONLY compatible fields\n    local trace = AgentsFrontend.trace\n    local request = trace.last_request\n    if request.endpoint ~= CompletionsEndpoints.oai_v1_chat_completions then\n        -- fail fast in this case\n        -- TODO (when I need it)... you very likely can support other endpoints (see what you've done in both PredictionsFrontend and RewriteFrontend (both have some multi endpoint support)\n        local message = \"AgentsFrontend SSEs not supported for endpoint: \" .. tostring(request.endpoint)\n        log:error(message)\n        vim.notify(message, vim.log.levels.ERROR)\n        return\n    end\n\n    if sse_parsed.choices == nil or sse_parsed.choices[1] == nil then\n        return\n    end\n    local first_choice = sse_parsed.choices[1]\n    AgentsFrontend.on_streaming_delta_update_message_history(first_choice, request, sse_parsed)\n    update_chat_viewer_buffer(trace)\nend\n\nfunction AgentsFrontend.show_user_role_as_follow_up_hint()\n    local lines_builder = LinesBuilder:new()\n    lines_builder:create_marks_namespace()\n    lines_builder:append_role_header(\"user\")\n    lines_builder:append_blank_line()\n    AgentsFrontend.chat_window:append_styled_lines(lines_builder)\nend\n\n---@type OnCurlExitedSuccessfully\nfunction AgentsFrontend.on_curl_exited_successfully()\n    vim.schedule(function()\n        -- FYI primary interaction (seam) between RxAccumulatedMessage and TxChatMessage (for assistant messages)\n        local trace = AgentsFrontend.trace -- FYI could pass trace from backend that calls on_curl_exited_successfully()\n        local request = trace.last_request\n        for _, rx_message in ipairs(request.accumulated_model_response_messages or {}) do\n            -- *** trace.last_request.accumulated_model_response_messages IS NOT trace.messages\n            --    trace.messages => sent with future requests, hence TxChatMessage\n            --    request.response_messages is simply to denormalize responses from SSEs, hence RxAccumulatedMessage\n            --    request => SSEs => RxAccumulatedMessage(s)  => toolcalls/followup => trace.messages (TxChatMessage) => next request => ...\n\n            -- add assistant response message to chat history (TxChatMessage)\n            --   (must come before tool result messages)\n            --   theoretically there can be multiple messages, with any role (not just assitant)\n            local trace_message = TxChatMessage:from_assistant_rx_message(rx_message)\n            trace:add_message(trace_message)\n            completion_logger.append_to_messages_jsonl(trace_message, request, AgentsFrontend)\n            -- TODO capture *-trace.json here too? and then get rid of response_message hack cuz all messages will now be in trace\n            --    TODO and careful to mirror changes (i.e. if move here, then need trace to save still for other frontends)\n\n            AgentsFrontend.show_user_role_as_follow_up_hint()\n\n            AgentsFrontend.chat_window.followup_starts_at_line_0indexed = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer:get_line_count() - 1\n        end\n        AgentsFrontend.clear_undos()\n\n        AgentsFrontend.run_tools_and_send_results_back_to_the_model(trace)\n    end)\nend\n\n---@param trace AgentTrace\nfunction AgentsFrontend.run_tools_and_send_results_back_to_the_model(trace)\n    local request = trace.last_request\n    for _, rx_message in ipairs(request.accumulated_model_response_messages or {}) do\n        for _, tool_call in ipairs(rx_message.tool_calls) do\n            -- FYI primary interaction (seam) between RxAccumulatedMessage and TxChatMessage (for tool result messages)\n\n            ---@type ToolCallDoneCallback\n            local function when_this_tool_is_done(tool_call_output)\n                -- * store output on rx_message\n                tool_call.call_output = ToolCallOutput:new(tool_call_output)\n                log:trace(\"tool_call_output\", vim.inspect(tool_call_output))\n\n                -- * triggers UI updates to show tool results\n                update_chat_viewer_buffer(trace)\n\n                -- * map tool result to a new TxChatMessage (to send back to model)\n                local tool_response_message = TxChatMessage:tool_result(tool_call)\n                -- log:jsonify_compact_trace(\"tool_message:\", tool_response_message)\n                tool_call.response_message = tool_response_message\n                trace:add_message(tool_response_message)\n\n                if request:any_outstanding_tool_calls() or request.already_sent then\n                    return\n                end\n                request.already_sent = true\n\n                -- IIUC I need to queue this after the changes from update_chat_viewer_buffer?\n                -- else IIRC, the line count will be broken for the next message\n                vim.schedule(function() AgentsFrontend.then_get_assistant_response(trace) end)\n            end\n\n            local function on_tool_progress(progress)\n                -- TODO show progress in chat viewer?\n                log:info(string.format(\"MCP tool progress [%s]: %s\", tool_call[\"function\"].name, vim.inspect(progress)))\n            end\n\n            -- * run the tool!\n            tool_router.send_tool_call_router(tool_call, when_this_tool_is_done, on_tool_progress)\n        end\n    end\nend\n\nfunction AgentsFrontend.abort_request()\n    local trace = AgentsFrontend.trace\n    if not trace then\n        return\n    end\n    CurlRequestForTrace.terminate(trace.last_request)\n    if AgentsFrontend.rag_cancel then\n        AgentsFrontend.rag_cancel()\n    end\nend\n\nfunction AgentsFrontend.follow_up_command()\n    -- take follow up after end of prior response message from assistant\n    --  if already a M.trace then add to that with a new message\n    --  leave content as is in the buffer, close enough to what it would be if redrawn\n    --  and I don't use the buffer contents for past messages\n    --  so, just copy it out into a new message from user\n    AgentsFrontend.ensure_chat_window_is_open()\n    local start_line_base0 = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.followup_starts_at_line_0indexed or 0\n    local user_message = AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer:get_lines_after(start_line_base0)\n    AgentsFrontend.chat_window.buffer:scroll_cursor_to_end_of_buffer()\n    vim.cmd(\"normal! o\") -- move to end of buffer, add new line below to separate subsequent follow up response message\n    -- log:trace(\"follow up content:\", user_message)\n\n    local trace = AgentsFrontend.trace\n    if not trace then\n        opts = {\n            -- simulate calling the same as the nvim AskAgent user command:\n            args = user_message\n        }\n        -- TODO if /selection that won't work, I can fix that later\n        --   TODO can't I get a history of selections or smth?\n        --    what if I wanna select text in the chat window itself?\n        --    does last selection track the file? is it per window?\n        -- hack: close window so LSP is available AND selections work (last cd ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim && python3 << 'PYTHON'
with open('lua/ask-openai/agents/viewer/formatters/generic.lua', 'r') as f:

# Add the render_progress function before the M.format function
render_progress_func = '''
---@param lines LinesBuilder
---@param tool_call ToolCall
---@param progress_messages string[]
---@param is_done boolean
local function render_progress(lines, tool_call, progress_messages, is_done)
    if is_done then
        return
    end

    -- Show current progress message (most recent) with spinner
    local spinner_chars = { "⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏" }
    local current_time = vim.loop.hrtime()
    local spinner_index = math.floor((current_time / 100000000) % #spinner_chars) + 1
    local spinner_char = spinner_chars[spinner_index]

    -- Show header with tool name and spinner
    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name or "unknown_tool"
    lines:append_line(string.format("%s ⏳ Running: %s", spinner_char, func_name))

    -- Show recent progress messages (up to 3 most recent)
    local num_progress = #progress_messages
    if num_progress > 0 then
        local max_show = math.min(3, num_progress)
        local start_idx = num_progress - max_show + 1

        if max_show < num_progress then
            lines:append_line(string.format("  ... (%d more messages)", num_progress - max_show))
        end

        for i = start_idx, num_progress do
            local msg = progress_messages[i]
            -- Truncate very long messages to avoid overwhelming the view
            if #msg > 200 then
                msg = msg:sub(1, 197) .. "..."
            end
            lines:append_line(string.format("    ↳ %s", msg))
        end
    else
        lines:append_line("    ↳ Waiting for response...")
    end
end

'''

# Insert before the M.format function
content = content.replace(
    '@---@type ToolCallFormatter\nfunction M.format',
    render_progress_func + '@---@type ToolCallFormatter\nfunction M.format'
)

# Replace the old "tool call in progress" section with the new progress rendering
old_section = '''    -- * tool result
    if not tool_call:is_done() then
        -- PRN for slow tools, use thinking dots ... as calling dots!
        lines:append_unexpected_line("Tool call in progress...")
        return
    end'''

new_section = '''    -- * progress messages (shown when tool is still running)
    local is_tool_done = tool_call:is_done()
    render_progress(lines, tool_call, tool_call.progress_messages or {}, is_tool_done)

    if not is_tool_done then
        return
    end'''

content = content.replace(old_section, new_section)

with open('lua/ask-openai/agents/viewer/formatters/generic.lua', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print("Applied changes to generic.lua")

cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/agents/viewer/formatters/generic.lua << 'HEREDOC'
local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local HLGroups = require("ask-openai.hlgroups")
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")

local M = {}

function try_decode_json_string(json_str, message)
    if message:is_still_streaming() then
        return json_str
    end

    local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json(json_str)
    if ok and type(decoded) == "table" then
        return vim.inspect(decoded, { newline = "", indent = "  " })
    end
    return json_str
end

---@param content string
---@param language? string
---@return string
local function code(content, language)
    local lang_prefix = language and #language > 0 and language or ""
    return "```" .. lang_prefix .. "\n" .. content .. "\n```"
end

---@param args string
---@param message RxAccumulatedMessage
---@return string text -- note, not lines
local function handle_apply_patch_args(args, message)
    if message:is_done_streaming() then
        -- FYI this is the only time to log failures, when done streaming:
        local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json(args)
        if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
            return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
        end
        -- * fallback is to show unparsed, final args
        -- also add a warning that will show in the chat window
        return args .. "\n\nFailed to decode as JSON"
    end

    local json_prefix = args:match('^{%s*"patch"%s*:%s*"')
    if not json_prefix then
        return args
    end

    -- * try to complete the JSON string and decode it
    -- assumption is, if final '"' + '}' is present then it would be done streaming (sans maybe one delta?)
    local try_finish_args = args .. '"}'
    local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json_ignore_failure(try_finish_args)
    if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
        return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
    else
        -- * fallback => try parse as-is
        local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json_ignore_failure(args)
        if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
            return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
        end
        -- * fallback => return unparsed
        return args
    end
end

---@param lines LinesBuilder
---@param tool_call ToolCall
---@param progress_messages string[]
---@param is_done boolean
local function render_progress(lines, tool_call, progress_messages, is_done)
    if is_done then
        return
    end

    -- Show current progress message (most recent) with spinner
    local spinner_chars = { "⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏" }
    local current_time = vim.loop.hrtime()
    local spinner_index = math.floor((current_time / 100000000) % #spinner_chars) + 1
    local spinner_char = spinner_chars[spinner_index]

    -- Show header with tool name and spinner
    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name or "unknown_tool"
    lines:append_line(string.format("%s ⏳ Running: %s", spinner_char, func_name))

    -- Show recent progress messages (up to 3 most recent)
    local num_progress = #progress_messages
    if num_progress > 0 then
        local max_show = math.min(3, num_progress)
        local start_idx = num_progress - max_show + 1

        if max_show < num_progress then
            lines:append_line(string.format("  ... (%d more messages)", num_progress - max_show))
        end

        for i = start_idx, num_progress do
            local msg = progress_messages[i]
            -- Truncate very long messages to avoid overwhelming the view
            if #msg > 200 then
                msg = msg:sub(1, 197) .. "..."
            end
            lines:append_line(string.format("    ↳ %s", msg))
        end
    else
        lines:append_line("    ↳ Waiting for response...")
    end
end

---@type ToolCallFormatter
function M.format(lines, tool_call, message)
    -- if message:is_still_streaming() then
    --     -- TODO?
    --     -- TODO OR message:get_lifecycle_step() == RxAccumulatedMessage.RX_LIFECYCLE.FINISHED
    --     return
    -- end

    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name
    local tool_header = func_name or ""

    local hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_SUCCESS
    if tool_call.call_output then
        if tool_call.call_output.result.isError then
            tool_header = "❌ " .. tool_header
            hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_FAILED
        else
            tool_header = "✅ " .. tool_header
        end
    end
    lines:append_styled_text(tool_header, hl_group)

    -- * tool args
    local args = tool_call["function"].arguments
    if args then
        if func_name == "apply_patch" then
            lines:append_text(handle_apply_patch_args(args, message))
        else
            lines:append_text(try_decode_json_string(args, message))
        end
    end
    -- PRN mark outputs somehow? or just dump them? (I hate to waste space)

    -- * progress messages (shown when tool is still running)
    local is_tool_done = tool_call:is_done()
    render_progress(lines, tool_call, tool_call.progress_messages or {}, is_tool_done)

    if not is_tool_done then
        return
    end

    -- TODO add failure recovery to building messages for logs... do not kill things b/c a formatter failed!
    --  allow agent to continue even if formatters are FUUUUU

    local is_mcp_like_output = tool_call.call_output and tool_call.call_output:is_mcp()
    if is_mcp_like_output then
        ---@type MCP_ContentBlock[]
        local content = tool_call.call_output.result.content

        local multiple_outputs = #content > 1
        for _, output in ipairs(content) do
            local name = output.name
            local text = tostring(output.text or "")
            if not text then
                -- PRN log and/or skip?
                if name then
                    lines:append_text(name)
                else
                    lines:append_text("[ empty text ]")
                end
            elseif name == "STDOUT" then
                if text then
                    lines:append_text_fold_if_long("STDOUT", text)
                end
            else
                -- GENERIC output type
                if (name == nil or name == "") and multiple_outputs then
                    name = "[NO NAME] and multiple outputs" -- heads up for now so I can identify scenarios/tools, can remove this later
                end
                if output.type == "text" then
                    local is_multi_line = text:match("\n")
                    if is_multi_line then
                        if name then
                            lines:append_text(name .. ":")
                        end
                        lines:append_text(text)
                    else
                        -- single line
                        if name then
                            lines:append_text(name .. ": " .. text)
                        else
                            lines:append_text(text)
                        end
                    end
                else
                    lines:append_unexpected_text("  UNEXPECTED type: \n" .. vim.inspect(output))
                end
            end
        end
    else
        -- TODO NON-MCP tool responses
        --  i.e. in-process tools: rag_query, apply_patch
    end
end

return M
HEREDOC
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

Done. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary

### Changes Made

1. **`tool_call.lua`** - Added `progress_messages` array field and `add_progress_message()` method to the `ToolCall` class. This accumulates progress messages during tool execution.

2. **`frontend.lua`** - Updated the `on_tool_progress` callback to:
   - Extract the `message` field from MCP progress notifications
   - Append it to `tool_call.progress_messages`
   - Trigger a buffer update via `update_chat_viewer_buffer()`

3. **`generic.lua`** - Added `render_progress()` function that displays:
   - Animated spinner (`⠋ ⠙ ⠹ ⠸ ⠼ ⠴ ⠦ ⠧ ⠇ ⠏`) cycling every ~100ms
   - `⏳ Running: tool_name` header
   - Up to 3 most recent progress messages (truncated at 200 chars)
   - `↳ Waiting for response...` when no messages yet
   - `... (N more messages)` indicator when >3 progress messages

4. **`hlgroups.lua`** - Added `TOOL_PROGRESS` highlight group (gold, italic) for future styling needs.

### How It Works

```
MCP Server → notifications/progress → progress_callbacks_by_token
    → on_progress callback (MCP/init.lua)
    → safe_on_progress (router.lua)
    → on_tool_progress (frontend.lua)
    → tool_call:add_progress_message(progress_message)
    → update_chat_viewer_buffer(trace)
    → formatter calls render_progress()
    → UI shows animated spinner + progress messages
```

...

local LinesBuilder = require("ask-openai.agents.viewer.lines_builder")
local ToolCall = require("ask-openai.agents.tools.tool_call")
local RxAccumulatedMessage = require("ask-openai.agents.messages.rx")

local M = {}

--- Render progress messages for an in-progress tool call.
--- Shows animated spinner, tool name, and up to 3 most recent progress updates.
--- When the tool is done, this is a no-op.
---
---@param lines LinesBuilder
---@param tool_call ToolCall
---@param is_done boolean
function M.render_progress(lines, tool_call, is_done)
    if is_done then
        return
    end

    -- Show current progress message (most recent) with spinner
    local spinner_chars = { "⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏" }
    local current_time = vim.loop.hrtime()
    local spinner_index = math.floor((current_time / 100000000) % #spinner_chars) + 1
    local spinner_char = spinner_chars[spinner_index]

    -- Show header with tool name and spinner
    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name or "unknown_tool"
    lines:append_line(string.format("%s ⏳ Running: %s", spinner_char, func_name))

    -- Show recent progress messages (up to 3 most recent)
    local num_progress = #tool_call.progress_messages
    if num_progress > 0 then
        local max_show = math.min(3, num_progress)
        local start_idx = num_progress - max_show + 1

        if max_show < num_progress then
            lines:append_line(string.format("  ... (%d more messages)", num_progress - max_show))
        end

        for i = start_idx, num_progress do
            local msg = tool_call.progress_messages[i]
            -- Truncate very long messages to avoid overwhelming the view
            if #msg > 200 then
                msg = msg:sub(1, 197) .. "..."
            end
            lines:append_line(string.format("    ↳ %s", msg))
        end
    else
        lines:append_line("    ↳ Waiting for response...")
    end
end

return M
HEREDOC
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local HLGroups = require("ask-openai.hlgroups")
local safely = require("ask-openai.helpers.safely")
local base = require("ask-openai.agents.viewer.formatters.base")

local M = {}

function try_decode_json_string(json_str, message)
    if message:is_still_streaming() then
        return json_str
    end

    local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json(json_str)
    if ok and type(decoded) == "table" then
        return vim.inspect(decoded, { newline = "", indent = "  " })
    end
    return json_str
end

---@param content string
---@param language? string
---@return string
local function code(content, language)
    local lang_prefix = language and #language > 0 and language or ""
    return "```" .. lang_prefix .. "\n" .. content .. "\n```"
end

---@param args string
---@param message RxAccumulatedMessage
---@return string text -- note, not lines
local function handle_apply_patch_args(args, message)
    if message:is_done_streaming() then
        -- FYI this is the only time to log failures, when done streaming:
        local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json(args)
        if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
            return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
        end
        -- * fallback is to show unparsed, final args
        -- also add a warning that will show in the chat window
        return args .. "\n\nFailed to decode as JSON"
    end

    local json_prefix = args:match('^{%s*"patch"%s*:%s*"')
    if not json_prefix then
        return args
    end

    -- * try to complete the JSON string and decode it
    -- assumption is, if final '"' + '}' is present then it would be done streaming (sans maybe one delta?)
    local try_finish_args = args .. '"}'
    local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json_ignore_failure(try_finish_args)
    if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
        return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
    else
        -- * fallback => try parse as-is
        local ok, decoded = safely.decode_json_ignore_failure(args)
        if ok and type(decoded) == "table" and decoded.patch then
            return code(decoded.patch, "diff")
        end
        -- * fallback => return unparsed
        return args
    end
end

---@type ToolCallFormatter
function M.format(lines, tool_call, message)
    local func_name = tool_call["function"].name
    local tool_header = func_name or ""

    local hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_SUCCESS
    if tool_call.call_output then
        if tool_call.call_output.result.isError then
            tool_header = "❌ " .. tool_header
            hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_FAILED
        else
            tool_header = "✅ " .. tool_header
        end
    end
    lines:append_styled_text(tool_header, hl_group)

    -- * tool args
    local args = tool_call["function"].arguments
    if args then
        if func_name == "apply_patch" then
            lines:append_text(handle_apply_patch_args(args, message))
        else
            lines:append_text(try_decode_json_string(args, message))
        end
    end
    -- PRN mark outputs somehow? or just dump them? (I hate to waste space)

    -- * progress messages (shown when tool is still running)
    local is_tool_done = tool_call:is_done()
    base.render_progress(lines, tool_call, is_tool_done)

    if not is_tool_done then
        return
    end

    -- TODO add failure recovery to building messages for logs... do not kill things b/c a formatter failed!
    --  allow agent to continue even if formatters are FUUUUU

    local is_mcp_like_output = tool_call.call_output and tool_call.call_output:is_mcp()
    if is_mcp_like_output then
        ---@type MCP_ContentBlock[]
        local content = tool_call.call_output.result.content

        local multiple_outputs = #content > 1
        for _, output in ipairs(content) do
            local name = output.name
            local text = tostring(output.text or "")
            if not text then
                -- PRN log and/or skip?
                if name then
                    lines:append_text(name)
                else
                    lines:append_text("[ empty text ]")
                end
            elseif name == "STDOUT" then
                if text then
                    lines:append_text_fold_if_long("STDOUT", text)
                end
            else
                -- GENERIC output type
                if (name == nil or name == "") and multiple_outputs then
                    name = "[NO NAME] and multiple outputs" -- heads up for now so I can identify scenarios/tools, can remove this later
                end
                if output.type == "text" then
                    local is_multi_line = text:match("\n")
                    if is_multi_line then
                        if name then
                            lines:append_text(name .. ":")
                        end
                        lines:append_text(text)
                    else
                        -- single line
                        if name then
                            lines:append_text(name .. ": " .. text)
                        else
                            lines:append_text(text)
                        end
                    end
                else
                    lines:append_unexpected_text("  UNEXPECTED type: \n" .. vim.inspect(output))
                end
            end
        end
    else
        -- TODO NON-MCP tool responses
        --  i.e. in-process tools: rag_query, apply_patch
    end
end

return M
HEREDOC
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>

---@return string: header
---@return table?: decoded_args
local function get_tool_header_text(args_json, message)
    if message:is_still_streaming() then
        return args_json, nil
    end

    local success, object_or_error = safely.decode_json_always_logged(args_json)
    if not success then
        return "json decode failure (see logs): " .. vim.inspect(object_or_error), nil
    end

    local command_line = object_or_error.command_line
    if command_line then
        object_or_error.command_line = nil
        return command_line, object_or_error
    end

    local argv = object_or_error.argv
    if argv then
        object_or_error.argv = nil
        local cmd = table.concat(argv, " ")
        return cmd, object_or_error
    end

    log:error("missing both argv and command_line", args_json)
    return "missing both argv and command_line: " .. args_json, object_or_error
end

---@type ToolCallFormatter
local function add_tool_header(lines, tool_call, message)
    local header, decoded_args = get_tool_header_text(tool_call["function"].arguments, message)
    local hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_SUCCESS
    if tool_call.call_output then
        if tool_call.call_output.result.isError then
            header = "❌ " .. header
            hl_group = HLGroups.TOOL_FAILED
        else
            header = "✅ " .. header
        end
    end
    -- gptoss sometimes uses a heredoc for a python script
    --   with \n between python statements
    --   stuffed in the command field!
    --   (anything to not use the stdin arg, lol)
    lines:append_styled_text(header, hl_group)
    return decoded_args
end

---@type ToolCallFormatter
function M.format(lines, tool_call, message)
    local decoded_args = add_tool_header(lines, tool_call, message)
    if decoded_args then
        -- PRN add indication that these are inputs? right now they blend in with outputs... (color?)
        if decoded_args.stdin then
            lines:append_text_fold_if_long("STDIN", decoded_args.stdin)
            decoded_args.stdin = nil
        end

        for key, value in pairs(decoded_args) do
            -- other inputs: cwd, stdin, timeout_ms, dry_run (I'd be fine w/ hiding timeout_ms and dry_run if false)
            lines:append_text(key .. ": " .. vim.inspect(value))
        end
    end

    -- -- debug dump everything:
    -- lines:append_text(vim.inspect(tool_call))

    -- * progress messages (shown when tool is still running)
    local is_tool_done = tool_call:is_done()
    base.render_progress(lines, tool_call, is_tool_done)

    if not is_tool_done then
        return
    end

    -- * tool result
    local is_mcp = tool_call.call_output.result.content
    if is_mcp then
        --- https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/server/tools#tool-result

        -- * TESTING:
        -- - date command is one liner
        -- - `ls -R` for lots of output

        ---@type MCP_ContentBlock[]
        local content = tool_call.call_output.result.content

        for _, output in ipairs(content) do
            local name = output.name
            local text = tostring(output.text or "")
            -- PRN dict. lookup of formatter functions by type (name), w/ registerType(), esp. as the list of types grows
            -- run_process
            if name == "STDOUT" then
                -- TODO! I want tool specific formatters... b/c for run_command, I want the command (esp if its small) to be the header! I don't need to see run_command ever, right?
                if text then
                    lines:append_text_fold_if_long("STDOUT", text)
                else
                    -- PRN skip STDOUT entirely if empty?
                    lines:append_unexpected_line("UNEXPECTED empty STDOUT?")
                end
            else
                -- GENERIC output type
                if name == nil or name == "" then
                    name = "[NO NAME]" -- heads up for now so I can identify scenarios/tools, can remove this later
                end
                if output.type == "text" then
                    local is_multi_line = text:match("\n")
                    if is_multi_line then
                        lines:append_text(name)
                        lines:append_text(text)
                    else
                        -- single line
                        lines:append_text(name .. ": " .. text)
                    end
                else
                    lines:append_unexpected_text("  UNEXPECTED type: \n" .. vim.inspect(output))
                end
            end
        end
    else
        -- TODO NON-MCP tool responses
        --  i.e. in-process tools: rag_query, apply_patch
    end
end

return M
HEREDOC
</parameter>
</function>

json-array ::= "[" space ("]" | json-value ("," space json-value)* space "]") space
json-bool ::= ("true" | "false") space
json-null ::= "null" space
json-number ::= "-"? ("0" | [1-9] [0-9]*) ("." [0-9]+)? (("e" | "E") [+-]? [0-9]+)? space
json-object ::= "{" space ("}" | json-string space ":" space json-value (space "," space json-string space ":" space json-value)* space "}") space
json-string ::= """ ( [^"\] | "\" ( ["\/ bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4} ) )* """ space
json-value ::= json-object | json-array | json-string | json-number | json-bool | json-null
number ::= ("-"? integral-part) ("." decimal-part)? ([eE] [-+]? integral-part)? space
root ::= tool-call
space ::= | " " | "
"{1,2} [ 	]{0,20}

tool-run-process-arg-argv ::= ("<parameter=" "argv" ">
